summary: TWO_COLUMN change-of-basis example. Left: an example vector u=[2,-1] is first built from standard basis scalars 2 and -1. Then P's columns are shown as the moved basis vectors b1=P i and b2=P j, the same scalars build 2b1-b2, and finally the whole standard grid is transformed by P so the moved grid, basis vectors, and vector overlap their target ghosts. Right: staged panel cards keep the scalar, column, and whole-grid interpretations separate.
